name: Nicotinamide for skin cancer prevention
summary: In patients with a history of nonmelanoma skin cancer, oral nicotinamide (vitamin B3) for 12 months reduced new nonmelanoma skin cancers by about 23%, with similar reductions in basal- and squamous-cell carcinomas and in actinic keratoses. It is inexpensive, over-the-counter, and well tolerated, but the benefit fades once it is stopped.
description: Nicotinamide (vitamin B3) aids DNA repair and reduces UV immunosuppression. The phase 3 ONTRAC trial randomized 386 high-risk patients (≥2 prior nonmelanoma skin cancers) to nicotinamide 500 mg twice daily or placebo for 12 months. The effect was not maintained after stopping and is not established in average-risk people.
interpretation: Outcomes are lesion rate ratios, so NNTs are per skin cancer prevented over 12 months—strikingly low here because high-risk patients get many lesions, and not transferable to average-risk people. The benefit disappears within months of stopping. Evidence is a single phase 3 trial; the basal-cell result was not significant and squamous-cell was borderline.
methodologyNotes: Rate ratios from ONTRAC's reported relative differences, converted to risk ratios; the control lesion rate is the NNT baseline. Actinic keratoses also fell (13% at 12 months) but lacked a reported CI, so are not encoded. Single trial (n=386).

outcomes.label: New nonmelanoma skin cancers
outcomes.description: New basal-cell plus squamous-cell carcinomas (primary endpoint).

studies.author: ONTRAC (Chen)
studies.citation: Chen AC, Martin AJ, Choy B, et al. A Phase 3 Randomized Trial of Nicotinamide for Skin-Cancer Chemoprevention (ONTRAC). N Engl J Med. 2015;373(17):1618-1626.
studies.notes: New nonmelanoma skin cancers (primary). Rate ratio from 23% lower (95% CI 4-38), P=0.02. Control rate 2.4 lesions/person/12 mo; NNT is per cancer prevented over 12 months.

studies.author: ONTRAC (Chen)
studies.citation: Chen AC, Martin AJ, Choy B, et al. A Phase 3 Randomized Trial of Nicotinamide for Skin-Cancer Chemoprevention (ONTRAC). N Engl J Med. 2015;373(17):1618-1626.
studies.notes: Basal-cell carcinoma. Rate ratio from 20% lower (95% CI -6 to 39), P=0.12 (not significant). Reduction was mainly in superficial BCCs.

studies.author: ONTRAC (Chen)
studies.citation: Chen AC, Martin AJ, Choy B, et al. A Phase 3 Randomized Trial of Nicotinamide for Skin-Cancer Chemoprevention (ONTRAC). N Engl J Med. 2015;373(17):1618-1626.
studies.notes: Squamous-cell carcinoma. Rate ratio from 30% lower (95% CI 0 to 51), P=0.05 (borderline).
